Skip to contents
vrpr 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference

vrpr

PyVRP for R — a production-grade vehicle routing problem (VRP) solver.

vrpr ports the PyVRP solver (a high-performance, MIT-licensed C++ core) to an idiomatic R package in the tidyverse style: a pipe-friendly API, data in tibbles, logging via {cli}, and the C++ core rewired with {cpp11} — with no Python runtime dependency.

It fills a real gap in the R ecosystem: there are ompr, ROI, lpSolve, igraph and dodgr, but no modern, strong, friendly solver for production VRP.

Status

🚧 Early development — all PyVRP variants already solve end-to-end: CVRP, VRPTW (time windows), heterogeneous fleet, MDVRP (multiple depots), prize-collecting (optional clients + groups), simultaneous pickup and delivery / backhaul, and multi-trip. Next: vignettes, CI, CRAN.

Parity with PyVRP is verified (see tools/benchmark/): the objective is identical bit for bit and, on the X-n101-k25 instance (optimum 27591), both reach the optimum in 10 s.

library(vrpr)

clients <- tibble::tibble(
  x = c(10, 25, 40, 15), y = c(5, 30, 12, 22),
  demand = c(10, 15, 8, 12)
)

res <- vrp_model() |>
  add_depot(x = 0, y = 0) |>
  add_clients(clients) |>
  add_vehicle_type(num_available = 3, capacity = 50) |>
  vrp_solve(stop = max_runtime(2))

cost(res)      # objective cost
routes(res)    # tibble: route_id, position, client, vehicle_type
summary(res)   # one-row summary

Variants

  • VRPTW: add tw_early, tw_late, service to clients; routes() reports start_service and wait per visit.
  • Heterogeneous fleet: call add_vehicle_type() several times with different capacities, costs (fixed_cost, unit_distance_cost) and shifts.
  • MDVRP: call add_depot() several times and use add_vehicle_type(depot = i) to base each vehicle type at a depot; routes() reports the depot column.
  • Prize-collecting: mark clients with required = FALSE and prize; the solver decides which to visit. add_client_group() defines mutually exclusive alternatives. See the skipped ones with unvisited_clients().
  • Pickup & delivery / backhaul: add a pickup column to clients (the collected load counts toward capacity along the route).
  • Multi-trip: add_vehicle_type(reload_depots = i, max_reloads = k) lets a vehicle return to a depot to reload and make several trips (summary()$num_trips).

Read standard instances 

m <- read_vrplib(system.file("extdata", "sample-n6-k2.vrp", package = "vrpr"))
m <- read_solomon(system.file("extdata", "sample-solomon.txt", package = "vrpr"))
res <- m |> vrp_solve(stop = max_runtime(5))

read_vrplib() reads CVRP/VRPTW in VRPLIB/TSPLIB format (EUC_2D); read_solomon() reads VRPTW in Solomon format. Both return a vrpr_model.

Visualise 

plot(res)   # depots, clients and routes (one colour per route) with {ggplot2}

plot() (on a result or a model) draws depots (squares), clients (points; unvisited optional ones appear hollow) and the coloured routes. Requires ggplot2.

Algorithm

Iterated Local Search (ILS) — inherited from PyVRP v0.13.x.

License

MIT. Derived from PyVRP (© PyVRP authors), whose copyright is preserved (see inst/COPYRIGHTS).
